prompt:
You are a research agent. Conduct thorough research on the following sub-topic.

## Research Sub-Topic
**Title**: Evaluation Results v1.4–v1.8: Quantitative Metrics and Version-by-Version Progress
**Description**: Research and analyze the evaluation results across UMAF versions v1.4 through v1.8. Document the test infrastructure growth from 42 smoke tests (v1.5) to 379 tests (v1.8) across 10 test files (conftest, test_smoke, test_pipeline, test_coder, test_research, test_coderpp, test_topology, test_skill, test_feature, test_self_evolution). Analyze v1.4 results: 7/7 workers (100% success rate), scores 48-38/50, 60KB LaTeX output, 443s pipeline time. Trace version-by-version improvements: v1.4.1 (8 bug fixes, 15 smoke tests), v1.5 (Topology + Skill pipelines, 42 smoke tests), v1.6 (Feature pipeline, 99 tests), v1.6.1 (dependency injection fixes, 131 tests), v1.7 (tools_config.json + deduplication, 99 tests), v1.8 (SelfEvolution + 175 new behavioral tests, 379 tests). Include the v1.4.1 agent loop fixes (tool execution before TASK_COMPLETE check, force wrap-up strengthening, post-loop forced write, error spiral threshold 3→2).

## Dependency Outputs (READ THESE FIRST)
Your research builds on the following completed sub-tasks. Read each file before starting your own research — do NOT redo work that has already been done. Instead, use these findings as a foundation and focus on what's missing or needs deeper analysis.

- **[4] CoderPipeline and CoderPPPipeline: Single-File and Multi-File Code Generation with Review Loops**: `research_04_CoderPipeline_and_CoderPPPipeline:_Single-File_and_Multi-Fil.md`
- **[5] ResearchPipeline: Dependency-Ordered Workers, Version-Bump Retries, and LaTeX Report Generation**: `research_05_ResearchPipeline:_Dependency-Ordered_Workers,_Version-Bump_R.md`
- **[6] TopologyPipeline and SkillPipeline: Agent Topology Optimization and Artifact-Agnostic Skill Detection**: `research_06_TopologyPipeline_and_SkillPipeline:_Agent_Topology_Optimizat.md`
- **[7] FeaturePipeline and SelfEvolutionPipeline: Project-Aware Code Generation and Autonomous Self-Improvement**: `research_07_FeaturePipeline_and_SelfEvolutionPipeline:_Project-Aware_Cod.md`

## Instructions
1. FIRST, use **Read** to read the dependency outputs listed above (`research_04_CoderPipeline_and_CoderPPPipeline:_Single-File_and_Multi-Fil.md`, `research_05_ResearchPipeline:_Dependency-Ordered_Workers,_Version-Bump_R.md`, `research_06_TopologyPipeline_and_SkillPipeline:_Agent_Topology_Optimizat.md`, `research_07_FeaturePipeline_and_SelfEvolutionPipeline:_Project-Aware_Cod.md`). These are completed research files from sub-tasks that yours depends on. Do NOT redo their work — build on it.
2. Use **WebSearch** to find additional papers, articles, and technical documentation. Run multiple searches with different query angles.
3. For any arxiv.org or academic URLs found in search results: do NOT try to fetch them directly (domain verification will block them). Instead, note the URLs in your findings so the framework can download them later.
4. Synthesize your findings using your own reasoning. Identify patterns, compare approaches, and note trade-offs.
5. Use **Write** to save your complete findings to the file: `research_09_Evaluation_Results_v1.4–v1.8:_Quantitative_Metrics_and_Versi.md`
   Organize your findings into these sections:
   - **Overview**: 2-3 paragraph summary covering the core ideas and motivation
   - **Key Methods & Approaches**: detailed technical explanation of how each method works, with comparisons
   - **Important Papers & References**: list key papers with authors, venue, year, and a 1-2 sentence note on their contribution
   - **Open Questions & Future Directions**: current limitations and active research frontiers
   - **Relevance to Main Topic**: how this connects to broader research
6. After writing, use **Read** to verify the file was written correctly with all sections present.
7. Output TASK_COMPLETE when done.

Focus on technical depth, concrete details, and accuracy. Your research will be scored and ranked on depth, accuracy, relevance, clarity, and originality.
response:
The research file is complete — 363 lines, ~7,055 words, ~55KB. All five required sections are present and well-developed:

- **Overview** (3 paragraphs): Dual-layer verification architecture, four-phase evolution, comparison with MultiAgentBench, AgentDevel, Collab-Overcooked, and State-Harness
- **Key Methods & Approaches** (6 major subsections): Test infrastructure architecture from monolith to 10-file modular suite, behavioral test design with causal chain verification, version-by-version quantitative and qualitative analysis (v1.4 through v1.8), agent loop reliability mechanisms with detailed before/after pseudocode, quantitative metrics trajectory tables, and evaluation architecture comparison with 6 rival frameworks
- **Important Papers & References** (17 papers): Self-Refine, Reflexion, ReAct, MultiAgentBench, AgentDevel, Collab-Overcooked, Benchmark Self-Evolving, MemEvolve, PACE, SAGE, Socratic-SWE, Meta-Agent Challenge, Godel Agent, Diagrid durability analysis, Compound AI behavior evaluation, State-Harness, Apple Agentic RAG Testing
- **Open Questions & Future Directions** (12 items): Test coverage gaps, self-evolution effectiveness, cross-model evaluation, reviewer calibration, adaptive test prioritization, adversarial test generation, inter-pipeline consistency, evaluation metric evolution, cost-evaluation tradeoffs, test suite maintenance, production-to-test feedback loops, comparative backend evaluation
- **Relevance to Main Topic**: UMAF's evaluation trajectory as a replicable case study for multi-agent LLM framework maturation

TASK_COMPLETE
